content.problemStatement:
This problem is similar to the problem `"STRFLIP2"`. The only difference between them is the number of moves allowed — in this problem, up to NNN moves can be made. In Div. 1 and Div. 2 this problem is worth 404040 points and `"STRFLIP2"` is worth 606060. In Div. 3 this problem is worth 100100100 points and `"STRFLIP2"` is non-scorable.

You are given a binary string AAA of length NNN, which we treat as being 111-indexed.

In one move, you are allowed to


Pick a **contiguous** substring (i.e. A[L:R]A[L:R]A[L:R] for some 1≤L≤R≤N1 \leq L \leq R \leq N1≤L≤R≤N) of AAA that contains **at least one** '000' and **at least one** '111'
Flip all the characters of the chosen substring (i.e. change every 000 to 111 and every 111 to 000)
You are also given a target string BBB of the same length as AAA.

Determine if it is possible to convert AAA to BBB by applying a sequence of moves as described above.

If it is possible to convert AAA to BBB, then find a sequence of no more than NNN moves that accomplishes this.
It can be shown that whenever it is possible to convert AAA to BBB, then it is possible to do so using no more than NNN moves.

If there are multiple answers, you can print any of them.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
Each test case consists of three lines.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, the size of the strings.
The second line of each test case contains AAA, a binary string with NNN characters.
The third line of each test case contains BBB, a binary string with NNN characters.


Output Format

For each test case, print the answer starting from a new line in the following format:
In the first line, print −1-1−1 if it is impossible to convert AAA to BBB. Otherwise, print a single non-negative integer KKK — the number of moves that your solution requires.
Use the next KKK lines to describe your sequence of moves, in the same order they are to be performed.
The ithi^{th}ith of these KKK lines should contain two integers LiL_iLi​ and RiR_iRi​, denoting the starting and ending positions of the substring chosen in the ithi^{th}ith operation.
Note that the following conditions must be satisfied:

0≤K≤N0 \leq K \leq N0≤K≤N
1≤Li≤Ri≤N1 \leq L_i \leq R_i \leq N1≤Li​≤Ri​≤N for each valid iii
Each substring A[Li:Ri]A[L_i : R_i]A[Li​:Ri​] must contain at least one '000' and one '111'.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all testcases does not exceed 500050005000
AAA and BBB are binary strings, i.e, contain only 000 and 111.
At most NNN moves can be made for each testcase.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Note: The sample tests use strings of small lengths just to illustrate the problem, and will not be present in the actual tests. Every string in the actual test files will have a length of at least 202020, as stated by the constraints.
Test Case 111: The given string is A=10110A = 10110A=10110. Apart from the single character substrings and the substring A[3:4]=11A[3:4] = 11A[3:4]=11, all other substrings are valid first moves.
The sample output performs moves as follows:

First, flip the substring A[1:4]A[1:4]A[1:4]. Now, A=01000A = 01000A=01000.
Then, flip the substring A[2:3]A[2:3]A[2:3]. Now, A=00100A = 00100A=00100, which is the target string.

Note that there are several other valid solutions, and any solution using no more than 555 moves will be accepted. For example, a sequence of 333 moves is shown below:

Flip A[1:5]A[1:5]A[1:5], giving A=01001A = 01001A=01001
Flip A[4:5]A[4:5]A[4:5], giving A=01010A = 01010A=01010
Flip A[2:4]A[2:4]A[2:4], giving A=00100A = 00100A=00100

Test Case 2: The given string A=00000A = 00000A=00000 has no substring with both 000 and 111, consequently there are no moves possible. Therefore it is impossible to convert this string to B=11111B = 11111B=11111.
Test Case 3: The given string A=00A = 00A=00 is already equal to BBB and so no moves are required.
